free_text: Measurement of the Cm-245(n,f) cross section in the
resolved resonance region.
J.Van Gils
O.Serot, B.Habert
C.Wagemans, S.Vermote
Proc.of Int.Workshop on Nuclear Fission and
Fission-product Spectroscopy.
ND2010 Conf.Proc. paper #1506;
in ND2010 Abstract book, page 65.
GELINA neutron facility of IRMM, Geel.
Repetition frequency of the accelerator 800 Hz.
Pulsed, compressed electron beam hits
rotating U target, producing bremsstrahlung gamma rays,
created neutrons via (gamma,n) and (gamma,f) reactions.
Neutrons are then moderated in 4 cm thick water-filled
beryllium containers.
Cadmium filter is placed in front of the chamber to
remove overlapping neutrons from previous bursts.
Two Si-Au surface barrier detectors to detect
fission fragments from Cm and alphas from monitor.
Length of the flight path is 9.3 m.
Incident neutron energy was determined by TOF.
Two runs:
-With highly enriched U-235 sample - to determine the
detection geometry factors on both sides and to check
if the U-235(n,f) cross section can be reproduced.
Gave confidence in the good functioning of exp. setup.
-With Cm sample at the same detection geometry.
*To remove spontaneous fission contributions,
additional run with the beam closed was performed using
a pulse generator. Background measurement on the boron
side was done with the black resonances filters of Rh,
Au, Mn, W, Co.
For neutron flux determination
To detection geometry factors.
M.M.
Request for the data was sent 10.11.2010 to O.Serot.
M.M. Ref. J,KPS,59,1896,2011 was added.
Authors' data were added in Subent 002.
Subent 004 was added.
BIB information was added.
SD: Subent 003 deleted (data given just to
demonstrate the correctness of energy calibration by
using the well known cross section)
Curium oxide sample of 50 microg/cm**2 thickness,active
diameter 15 mm was mounted in the centre of a vacuum
chamber, back-to-back with B-10 layer.
Isotopic composition in March 2003 : Cm-245 - 98.48%,
The energy resolution was improve between 10 and 20 eV
compared to Browne+ measurement.
Nice agreement with Moore+ measurement.
White+ measurement shows a shift in energy due to
a problem of energy calibration.
CONRAD code
New set of resonance parameters will be determined in
near future. Resolved resonance energy region (ends at
100 eV in Maslov's evaluation (adopted by JEFF3.1,
ENDF/B-VII.O, JENDL 3.3) can be probably extended.
Experimental data were analyzed with the CONRAD code
developed recently at CEA-Cadarache, to determine the
resonance parameters using the Reich-Moore formalism.
Not specified, probably total.
Data from O.Serot.
Preliminary results.
Data were received from Oliver Serot via
N.Otsuka (NDS, IAEA).
Curium oxide sample of 50 microg/cm**2 thickness,active
diameter 15 mm was mounted in the centre of a vacuum
chamber, back-to-back with B-10 layer.
Isotopic composition in March 2003 : Cm-245 - 98.48%,
RI are compared with those of:
Energy range,ev  JEFF-3.1  Browne  Moore  White
Between 0.5 eV and 100 eV the difference between
JEFF-3.1.1 and the present work is < 1%. Between 20 eV
and 100 eV our results show a more important systematic
underestimation (up to 15%) compared to JEFF-3.1.1.
Not specified, probably total.
Preliminary results.
Subent was added.
